problem: Why do traumatic memories become locked and intrusive — what neural mechanism?
hypothesis: Why do traumatic memories become locked and intrusive — what neural mechanism? remains unsolved because previous research missed the role of Ketamine-like. Ketamine-like NMDA antagonism without dissociation directly addresses this.
